extra.reason: All S1 acceptance criteria pass in-cluster: instantiation p99 25.3µs < 10ms; stable at 100 residents (0.47 MiB/component); clean 256MiB cap kill with host surviving. Raw numbers in docs/p0-results.md.

Carried patch shipped (user decision: no upstream PR). patches/0001 gives every wash-runtime store an epoch deadline (per-component wamn.epoch-deadline-ticks config > WAMN_EPOCH_DEADLINE_TICKS env > unbounded); epoch enable + 10ms ticker live unpatched in wamn-host (EngineBuilder::with_config layering + pub increment_epoch). Demonstrated locally and in-cluster: busyloop raw store killed at ~191-195ms of a 200ms deadline as Trap::Interrupt; busyloop service killed at config deadline with host heartbeat OK and new workloads accepted; hello/http and all S1 gates unaffected under the running ticker. Documented in docs/p0-results.md S1 follow-up. S2 chaos gate unblocked.

S2 shipped: real wamn:postgres plugin (tokio-postgres+deadpool) + pgbench harness + pgprobe guest. In-cluster gate of record ALL PASS: 20427 qps / p99 1.98ms (<10ms); graceful connection-unavailable at saturation; chaos 100/100 connections destroyed with clean claim-free/txn-free checkouts; RLS 0 leaks across 10k + 984/984 cross-tenant writes permission-denied; injection 0 mismatches across 10k. Fail branch not taken. Details in docs/p0-results.md S2.

Fixed: removed-entity DROP TABLEs now emit dependents-first — topologically ordered on inbound Reference edges within the removed set (Kahn rounds mirroring the k56 rename ordering) in emit.rs migrate_plan, replacing the FK-blind entity-id-lexical order. A mutual-FK cycle among dropped tables is rejected (new CompileError::DropCycle), consistent with the TableRenameCycle/ColumnRenameCycle v1 stance (constraint-drop-first deferred). Tests: 3 units (2-table fk-order, 3-chain multi-round, mutual-fk->DropCycle) + a dedicated live-apply gate (schema wamn_ddl_drop_order_test, real inbound FKs + live rows, asserts the whole-chain drop SUCCEEDS — was 2BP01). Lexical-order mutant killed by all 4 (live gate shows the exact 'constraint books_author_id_fkey depends on table authors'). cargo test/clippy/fmt -p wamn-ddl green; consumer regressions (schema/rls/seed/catalog) green. docs/ddl-compiler.md updated (step 5 dependents-first ordering + DropCycle; nqg removed from known limits). Pure-crate, no docker/in-cluster.

Fixed: emit_column_alters now maintains the reference-field FK lifecycle across a retype. A reference field's FK (<table>_<field>_fkey) is synthesized by fk_op and never modeled as a catalog constraint, so ALTER COLUMN TYPE did not carry it: a Reference->Uuid retype left log_aref_fkey behind, and a later DROP TABLE of the (removed) target failed 2BP01. Fix uses type_changed's Option<(old,new)> — when the type LEAVES Reference, emit ALTER TABLE ... DROP CONSTRAINT before the retype (drops by the pre-rename field name if the same bump renames it); when it ENTERS Reference, emit fk_op ADD CONSTRAINT after the retype; a Reference re-pointed at a new target does both. Added a shared fk_constraint_name() helper (fk_op refactored onto it) + drop_reference_fk_op(). Tests: 3 units (Reference->Uuid drop-before-DROP-TABLE, Uuid->Reference add-after-retype, Reference->Reference re-point drop+add) + a dedicated live-apply gate (own schema wamn_ddl_retype_fk_test, 2 scenarios: retype-away-drops-target succeeds [was 2BP01] with the referencing row surviving; retype-into-Reference adds a REAL enforcing FK proven by a rejected bogus insert). TWO mutants killed (neuter drop -> 3 drop-tests fail incl live 2BP01; neuter add -> 3 add-tests fail incl live foreign_key_violation-expected), each killed by exactly its half. cargo test/clippy/fmt -p wamn-ddl green (39 tests); consumer regressions (schema/rls/seed/catalog) green. docs/ddl-compiler.md documents the retype FK lifecycle + Verification section updated (5 live gates). Pure-crate, no docker/in-cluster. Residual (broader, out of scope): a table renamed in a PRIOR bump leaves the synthesized FK name reflecting the older table prefix — the same synthesized-name-drift family, unsolvable from (old,new) alone.

Structural empty-tenant floor hardening: NULLIF(current_setting('app.tenant',true),'') in USING+WITH CHECK + CHECK (tenant_id <> '') on the tenant_id column, in the 3.2 emitter (emit.rs rls_op/create_table_op) and all FIVE hand-written schemas (run-state, run-queue incl outbox, catalog-schema, postgres-init, flows — the bead's list missed flows.sql). wamn-seed compile() rejects empty/whitespace tenant (new CompileError::EmptyTenant). Converts the empty-claim-safe property from invariant to structural. Live-apply gate empty_tenant_claim_matches_no_row_on_postgres proves both halves independently (CHECK rejects a superuser '' insert; NULLIF hides a planted ''-row after the CHECK is dropped). 3 mutants killed. Query-level server-side tenant writes (router.rs INSERT, run-queue/run-store builders) deliberately left as-is — the CHECK backstops an empty-claim write.

Structural + edge exclusion of JSON-type-changing special values (NaN / ±infinity) from catalog-derived entity columns. Implemented in commit 87e9be7. Ramp retries the first saturated classification at a level and accepts only reproduced saturation; p99-doubling baseline = first ACCEPTED-good run (a poisoned first run can no longer become the baseline). Gates: harness units 12/12; cargo-mutants on ceiling.rs 49 caught / 8 missed — 5 pre-existing float boundaries in saturated(), 2 pre-existing shifted expressions (bisect tolerance, 0.9 drain factor), 1 equivalent mutant (baseline guard: confirmed-sat with no baseline forces Done, mutated write unobservable). Serves the whole v3 ceiling program (C-CDC/C-JS/C-MAT knee searches inherit the defense). Unblocks the tuning-matrix half of C-QUEUE (wamn-z7b.6, re-parented to wamn-l5i9).
